headline: Defenders harden the software supply chain and turn AI loose on their own code, even as the same technology fuels the phishing machines law enforcement keeps tearing down.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Dual Use
emerging_trends.text: Claude Opus 4.8 surfaced a critical Zcash flaw the same week the FBI dismantled an AI-powered phishing service, putting the technology on both sides of the fight.

emerging_trends.subject: Authentication Under Siege
emerging_trends.text: A jailed school-district insider with lingering admin rights and Chinese operators who owned a target's auth stack for a decade show attackers winning by controlling who gets to log in.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain Hardening
emerging_trends.text: npm's move to stop running dependency scripts by default attacks the install-time code execution behind a run of recent package compromises.

topics.title: npm 12 Disables Dependency Scripts by Default
topics.latest_developments: npm 12 will stop running install scripts from dependencies unless a developer explicitly allows each one.
topics.summary: Today npm install runs the lifecycle scripts a package defines, the exact mechanism attackers abuse to execute code the moment a developer pulls a poisoned dependency. Version 12 flips the default so those scripts stay dormant until a developer opts in per package. The change goes after the install-time execution behind a string of JavaScript ecosystem compromises. Teams should expect builds that genuinely need scripts to declare them.

sources.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks

topics.title: Claude Opus 4.8 Surfaces Critical Zcash Orchard Flaw
topics.latest_developments: Researcher Taylor Hornby, working with Claude Opus 4.8, found a critical vulnerability in the Zcash Orchard privacy pool on May 29, and the Zcash team fixed it.
topics.summary: Orchard, Zcash's newest privacy pool, arrived in 2022 to let users send shielded transactions. The Zcash team hired Hornby specifically to hunt for flaws, and he surfaced a critical one fast with help from Claude Opus 4.8. Developers patched it. Anyone holding Zcash should confirm they run the corrected software.

sources.title: Critical Zcash Vulnerability Found and Fixed

topics.title: Former School District IT Worker Jailed for Sabotage
topics.latest_developments: A court sentenced a former Iowa school district IT employee to 21 months in prison for a prolonged attack that deleted accounts and disrupted classrooms.
topics.summary: The former administrator kept access after leaving and used it to wage a drawn-out attack on the district, deleting accounts, halting classroom operations, and running up tens of thousands of dollars in damage. The 21-month sentence measures the harm a trusted insider with lingering credentials still inflicts. Organizations should cut departing staff access the day they leave and watch privileged accounts for abuse.

sources.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer

topics.title: New macOS Tahoe 26 Artifact Tracks User Menu Choices
topics.latest_developments: Unit 42 disclosed a previously unknown macOS Tahoe 26 forensic artifact that records the menu selections a user makes across the operating system.
topics.summary: Palo Alto's Unit 42 found that macOS Tahoe 26 logs user menu selections throughout the system, a trail investigators can mine to reconstruct what someone did during an incident. The artifact hands forensic analysts a finer record of intent on a Mac. Defenders writing macOS investigation playbooks should fold it into their collection routines.

sources.title: Tracing Digital Intent: New MacOS Tahoe 26 Artifact Discovered

weather: This Afternoon: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely, high 84F.
Tonight: Showers And Thunderstorms then Mostly Cloudy, low 56F.

games.recap: Spencer Horwitz hit by pitch with the bases loaded to lift the Pirates past the Marlins, 3-2
Meyer outduels Skenes, allows one run in six innings as Marlins top Pirates 4-2

business_politics.title: Trump Rebukes Israel Over Beirut Strike
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump publicly rebuked Israel and ordered Israel and Hezbollah to stand down after the Beirut strike, insisting the U.S.-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz can still close Sunday.
business_politics.summary: Israel hit what it called a Hezbollah command center on Beirut's outskirts, and Iran threatened to abandon the U.S. talks and retaliate. President Trump told both sides to halt attacks, racing to finalize an agreement that would end the U.S.-Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

sources.title: Trump Rebukes Israel Over Strike on Beirut
Trump tells Israel and Hizbollah to ‘stand down’ as he pushes for Iran deal

business_politics.title: China Launches Cross-Border Payments System to Rival Dollar
business_politics.latest_developments: Beijing unveiled a cross-border currency platform backed by the central banks of Hong Kong, Thailand, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia, its most concrete move yet to cut reliance on the dollar.
business_politics.summary: China teed up a digital payments system to settle cross-border transactions outside dollar channels, drawing in Gulf and Southeast Asian central banks. The platform advances Beijing's long campaign to internationalize the yuan and blunt the reach of U.S. financial sanctions.

sources.title: China tees up digital payments system to compete with dollar

business.title: Nara Organics Recalls Baby Formula
business.latest_developments: Nara Organics recalled infant formula sold at Target and online after a multistate infant botulism outbreak.
business.summary: Federal regulators linked the formula to infant botulism cases across several states. The recall covers products sold in Target stores and online, and parents should stop using affected lots.

sources.title: Nara Organics recalls baby formula sold at Target after multistate infant botulism outbreak
Baby formula sold at Target and online recalled after infant botulism cases reported

business.title: Pennsylvania Quarantines Farm Animals Over Screwworm
business.latest_developments: Pennsylvania issued a quarantine order on farm animals as it tracks New World screwworm cases spreading through southwestern states.
business.summary: The flesh-eating parasite, contained for decades, has resurged in the U.S. Pennsylvania's order restricts animal movement to keep the pest out of the state's livestock.

sources.title: Pa. issues quarantine order on farm animals, as wary states track screwworm cases in southwestern states

around_town.title: Hampton Township Road Project Restricts Traffic
around_town.latest_developments: A $10.57 million improvement project will restrict traffic on a Hampton Township roadway for more than a month.
around_town.summary: The work covers drainage upgrades, milling and paving, and base repair. Drivers face restrictions through the duration of the job.

sources.title: Improvement project to restrict traffic on Hampton Township roadway for over a month

around_town.title: Report Faults Pa. Teacher Reading Training
around_town.latest_developments: A new report found nearly half of Pennsylvania's educator preparation programs fail to adequately train future teachers in how to teach reading.
around_town.summary: The finding lands as states overhaul reading instruction through new laws. The gap touches the colleges that supply Pennsylvania's classroom teachers.

sources.title: Many Pa. colleges aren’t properly training future teachers how to teach reading, report finds

events.title: Pennsylvania Firefly Festival in Tionesta
events.latest_developments: The festival, June 26 and 27, opens free daytime programming even as its guided nocturnal firefly sightings have sold out.
events.summary: The 14th annual Pennsylvania Firefly Festival runs Friday and Saturday, June 26 and 27, in Tionesta, in the Allegheny National Forest, about 100 miles north of Pittsburgh. The guided nighttime viewings are booked, though free daytime nature exhibits, music, and activities remain open. The forest holds at least 15 firefly species, all glowing this time of year.

sources.title: What happens at a firefly festival?

around_teams.title: Oneil Cruz Out Four to Six Weeks
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pirates expect Oneil Cruz to miss four to six weeks and have recalled Esmerlyn Valdez to fill the roster spot.
around_teams.summary: Cruz's absence pulls a power bat from the Pirates lineup. Valdez gets the call-up to cover the opening.

sources.title: With Oneil Cruz expected to miss 4-6 weeks, the Pirates have recalled Esmerlyn Valdez

around_teams.title: Skenes Urges Players to Dig In on Labor Fight
around_teams.latest_developments: Paul Skenes, at the center of MLB's labor battle, said the players need to dig in ahead of a possible work stoppage.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette laid out how the Pirates ace views the fight over payroll, a salary cap, and the next collective bargaining agreement. Skenes argues players should hold firm against the owners.

sources.title: Why Paul Skenes, at the center of MLB's labor battle, believes the players need to dig in

around_teams.title: Ten Takeaways on McCarthy's Steelers
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette distilled 10 things it learned about Mike McCarthy and the Steelers during spring workouts, spanning Aaron Rodgers, Joey Porter Jr., DK Metcalf, and the backfield.
around_teams.summary: The team wrapped its 2026 offseason program this week. The lessons cover the new offense under McCarthy, the quarterback room, and roster competition heading toward training camp at Saint Vincent College.

sources.title: 10 things we learned about Mike McCarthy and the Steelers during spring workouts

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues Silicon Valley's AI era is ending, reading OpenAI's and Anthropic's moves to go public as a scramble for exit liquidity by companies that burn billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Ben Bajarin About Apple, AI, and Compute
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Ben Bajarin on Apple's WWDC, its AI strategy, and the state of the AI compute industry.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn’t AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport questions why AI has yet to eliminate jobs the way industrial automation did, challenging the analogy AI leaders draw between their technology and the machines that replaced human brawn.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
